title: Post-Holiday Family Device Purchase Dispute Pattern
narrative: Cardholder disputes $800-$2400 electronics purchase 45-75 days post-transaction, claiming unauthorized use. Investigation reveals transaction originated from device with matching device_id fingerprint to cardholder's previous 15+ authenticated purchases over 6 months. Purchase occurred during daytime hours (10AM-4PM) when cardholder historically shops, used stored CVV, shipped to card_addr1 matching billing, signed for at delivery. Email confirmation opened from same IP subnet (dist1 < 100km) as cardholder's residence within 3 hours of purchase. Chargeback filed precisely at 60-day mark, typical of coached disputes. Pattern common with teen/spouse purchases where family member used saved payment on shared device, then primary cardholder disputes upon discovering charge.
indicators: device_id matches cardholder's historical authenticated sessions (15+ prior txns)
Delivery addr1 exact match to card billing address with confirmed signature
Email opened from IP with dist1 < 100km from shipping address within 180 minutes
Chargeback filed at day 60-75, no contact with merchant prior
Transaction txn_hour between 10-16, matching cardholder's 80%+ purchase window
CVV match, card1_txn_count_24hr = 1, no velocity anomaly
summary: High-value electronics purchase shows all hallmarks of legitimate cardholder activity — same device used for 15+ prior purchases, delivery to billing address with signature, email opened nearby, transaction during customer's normal shopping hours — yet disputed at exactly 60 days with no prior merchant contact.
reasoning: Family member (teen or spouse) likely made the purchase on a shared device using saved payment info, and the primary cardholder disputes it upon seeing the statement rather than resolving it as a household matter, coached to wait until day 60 to maximize chargeback window.
indicator_explanations: Device is the cardholder's own trusted device with long transaction history
Purchase amount fits typical electronics price range for high-value consumer goods
Item was delivered to cardholder's verified billing address with signature confirmation
Cardholder opened delivery email from their home location shortly after shipment
Chargeback filed at maximum dispute window without any merchant communication attempts
Purchase made during cardholder's normal daytime shopping hours and behavior pattern
Card verification passed and transaction was isolated with no suspicious velocity
